note: The opening title card for the show — this is the pick (idea 1). The other nine ideas are further down under "Title card — other ideas".

title: Title card — idea 1 (chosen)
notes: Highest-resolution version. The title hand-writes on over the first part of the clip, then holds while the watercolor mist drifts. Note: ideas 3 and 6 are byte-for-byte the same file as this one.

title: Scene 1 — Holmes entrance / exit
notes: Holmes faces front, turns to profile, then walks frame-right and exits. Plain single-subject silhouette.

title: Scene 2 — Moriarty menacing (I)
notes: Moriarty stands menacing at the table, then walks frame-left and exits. Plain single-subject silhouette.

title: Scene 3 — Moriarty menacing (II)
notes: Moriarty enters frame-left, walks to centre, holds centre menacing/gesturing, then walks frame-right. Plain single-subject silhouette. Walk-in/out leg-gap timing de-popped so the legs separate on the same frame as the source instead of snapping open a couple frames late.

title: Scene 4 — Séance: Moriarty flickers to the Ghost
notes: Runway-flicker version, full: real moving Moriarty HARD-CUTS into the runway flicker transition (its glow/grain look kept), which HARD-CUTS into the real Ghost footage for the hood-off and playout. Both seams are size/position-matched so it reads as one continuous shot.

title: Scene 5 — The Falls: fight + falling deerstalker
notes: Moriarty solo, Holmes enters, the grapple at the falls, the deerstalker falls and lands, Holmes downed, Moriarty exits, Holmes returns alone. Single union silhouette.

title: Scene 6 — Moriarty reaches for Holmes
notes: Moriarty stands center and reaches his right arm toward off-frame Holmes. Plain single-subject silhouette.

note: The other nine title-card ideas, kept as alternates to the chosen one at the top. Each card lists its filename and specs, plus whatever I could verify is different. Heads-up: some are duplicate files — ideas 3 and 6 are the same render as idea 1, and idea 5 is the same as idea 2 — so they'll play identically.

title: Title card — idea 2
notes: 720p render. Byte-for-byte identical to idea 5.

title: Title card — idea 3
notes: Byte-for-byte identical to idea 1 (same render).

title: Title card — idea 4
notes: A separate 720p render that writes on and then holds, like idea 1. It's a distinct file, but I couldn't identify what visually distinguishes it from the other 10-second 720p versions.

title: Title card — idea 5
notes: Byte-for-byte identical to idea 2 (same render).

title: Title card — idea 6
notes: Byte-for-byte identical to idea 1 (same render).

title: Title card — idea 7
notes: Slower write-on: the lettering is still being drawn through most of the clip and finishes near the end, rather than completing early and holding like idea 1.

title: Title card — idea 8
notes: Slowest write-on: the title finishes drawing right at the very end — even slower than idea 7 (at the 7-second mark it's a full line behind idea 7).

title: Title card — idea 9
notes: A separate 720p render that writes on and then holds, like idea 1. It's a distinct file, but I couldn't identify what visually distinguishes it from the other 10-second 720p versions.

title: Title card — idea 10
notes: The short cut — half the length (121 frames vs 241), so the whole write-on plays in about 5 seconds instead of 10.

note: Filmed before we did it with a scene read. Some are pretty good and we may want to use them. Each is shown twice: the raw footage (for the performance) and its silhouette version. All cut from IMG_2158.MOV. Silhouette versions are pending projectionist review.

title: Clapping
notes: Raw footage (color).

title: Clapping — silhouette
notes: Silhouette of the raw clip above. He walks frame-right to frame-left and exits; legs read as an open/merged shadow through the stride.

title: Book
notes: Raw footage (color).

title: Book — silhouette
notes: Silhouette of the raw clip above. The held book reads in the silhouette.

title: Hand to face
notes: Raw footage (color).

title: Hand to face — silhouette
notes: Silhouette of the raw clip above.

title: Hand to face (take 2)
notes: Raw footage (color).

title: Hand to face (take 2) — silhouette
notes: Silhouette of the raw clip above.

title: Arms raised
notes: Raw footage (color).

title: Arms raised — silhouette
notes: Silhouette of the raw clip above. The wide arm-spread reads cleanly.

note: Alternatives explored for the Moriarty->Ghost transition (p.76). V9 (the runway hard-cut with the real moving Moriarty front + real Ghost hood-off back) is the chosen Scene 4 above. The rest are kept for reference: V8 same but without the moving front; V7 runway with a fade; V1-V3 flicker-out then pop; V4-V6 accelerating flicker.

title: Scene 4 — candidate V7: “Runway flicker” (fade version)
notes: First runway version: runway transition (look kept) DISSOLVES into the real Ghost footage. Superseded by V8, which aligns the figures and hard-cuts instead. Kept for comparison.

title: Scene 4 — candidate V1: “Dying bulb”
notes: He blinks fully OUT and back 3 times, the gaps widening each time, then he's gone — a short empty-stage beat — then the Ghost SNAPS in solid. Cleanest read; no ghost peek-through.

title: Scene 4 — candidate V2: “Two worlds”
notes: Same flicker-outs, but in the last gaps the Ghost shows THROUGH instead of white — the two figures phase man↔ghost, one last Moriarty flash 'fighting', then gone and the Ghost pops. Most dramatic; a touch busier.

title: Scene 4 — candidate V3: “Slow collapse”
notes: Only 2 long, slow flicker-outs and a longer fade to gone, then the Ghost MATERIALIZES (one quick blink) and holds. Most deliberate / séance-y.

title: Scene 4 — candidate V4: “Accelerando”
notes: The flicker accelerates smoothly — fast, then much faster — until he snaps out; a short empty beat, then the Ghost hard-pops in solid.

title: Scene 4 — candidate V5: “Two gears”
notes: Two distinct speeds: a few fast flickers, then a much-faster burst, then gone and the Ghost pops. Same idea as V4 but in two clear 'gears' instead of a smooth ramp.

title: Scene 4 — candidate V6: “Charge + materialize”
notes: Moriarty accelerates out (like V4), then the energy passes to the Ghost — she flickers IN fast and settles solid, rather than a single hard pop.
